name: Sourdough Bagels
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Sugar in 4 quarts boiling water
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Primary Sourdough Batter B according to recipe. Be sure that you have saved 1 cup starter separately. Let all ingredients come to room temperature.
2. Sift 1 1/2 cups flour, salt, and sugar into a warm bowl. Stir in salad oil and eggs.
3. Stir in the Primary Batter B and add enough additional flour for the dough to leave the sides of the bowl.
4. Turn dough onto a well-floured board and knead in enough additional flour to make the dough smooth and elastic (about 1/4 cup). Place in a warm greased bowl, cover, and set the bowl in a warm 85°F spot until doubled in bulk. This will take about 2 hours.
5. When doubled, punch down and let proof for an additional 1 1/2 hours or until doubled in bulk.
6. Turn dough out onto a floured board and divide it into 12-14 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a 6-inch roll about 3/4 inch thick.
7. Pinch the two ends together to form a doughnut shape. Boil 4 quarts of water and add 2 tablespoons sugar.
8. Drop each bagel into boiling water one at a time. Cook for only 2 minutes total; do not cook longer than 3 minutes or they will become tough.
9. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a greased cookie sheet. When all have been boiled, put in the preheated oven (375°F) and bake for 20-25 minutes until crusty and golden brown. Makes 12-14 bagels.
